design_philosophy.core_loop: Pilot combat missions in a branching campaign, receive performance-based rewards/penalties, manage wingman orders, complete objectives to progress
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Mission success/failure directly affects campaign trajectory and available resources; permanent wingman death creates narrative stakes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Becoming an elite space fighter pilot rising through military ranks in an ongoing interstellar war; comparable to Star Wars space combat fantasy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unforgiving mission branching system where failure compounds difficulty rather than resetting; players must live with consequences of poor performance. Story-driven simulation that prioritizes narrative campaign consequences over skill-reset mechanics. Characterized as 'World War II in space' with permanent crew loss.

difficulty.lives_system: Player survives mission failures to continue campaign, but wingmen can be permanently killed in combat
difficulty.continues_system: Campaign branches based on performance; failed objectives lead to increasingly difficult defensive missions rather than game over
difficulty.checkpointing: Mission-based checkpointing; player can fail objectives and continue campaign on alternate path
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through branching structure: players can fail missions to fall into 'losing' path or recover from losing path by succeeding at subsequent missions. Overall campaign difficulty scales with performance trajectory.

progression.upgrade_system: Rank-based progression grants access to better ships; successful missions result in promotions and ship upgrades; failed missions result in inferior ship assignments
progression.unlock_mechanism: Mission performance determines branching campaign paths. Success leads to higher-stakes missions and advanced ship access. Failure leads to defensive scenarios with older equipment.
replayability_hooks: Branching campaign structure creates multiple distinct campaign paths (winning vs losing branches)
Permanent wingman death creates unique campaign outcomes based on pilot survival
Two expansion packs provide alternative storylines without branching mechanics
Player naming and callsign selection personalizes protagonist

world_building_techniques: Branching mission briefings that reflect campaign performance and contextual story state
Protagonist rank progression mirrors story advancement through military hierarchy
Named wingmen with permanent death consequences create character attachment and narrative weight
Specific planets and scenarios unlock based on campaign branch (Venice system strike vs defensive retreat scenarios)
Expansion packs introduce new story elements: Graviton weapon discovery, Firekkan alliance negotiations, Kilrathi defections
Enemy faction characterization: Kilrathi portrayed as predatory, aggressive feline warrior race

boss_design.philosophy: Campaign-culminating capital ship encounters tied to narrative payoff rather than per-mission boss fights. Sivar-class dreadnought in Secret Missions represents final objective. Boss encounters serve as campaign climax rather than routine mission obstacles.
notable_bosses: Sivar-class dreadnought (Secret Missions expansion) - equipped with Graviton weapon, mounted in Venice system or Vigrid system depending on campaign branch
Kilrathi battle group (Secret Missions 2) - featuring Dralthi II fighters, Hhriss-class heavy fighters, Snakeir-class heavy carriers

faction_indicators: Terran Confederation as player faction (multinational human military alliance)
Kilrathi Empire as antagonist faction (feline warrior race, culturally aggressive)
Firekkans as neutral/allied faction (bird-like species negotiating confederation membership)
danger_signals: Unprecedented enemy ship classes appearing in sector (graviton-armed dreadnoughts, new fighter variants)
Massive Kilrathi battle group concentrations in previously stable systems
Colony destruction on unprecedented scale (quarter million colonist casualties)
Defecting Kilrathi lord indicating internal faction instability
environmental_storytelling: Wreckage fields indicating recent combat or massacres
Carrier-based mission staging reflects military hierarchy and resource allocation
Sector-wide battle progression from offensive strikes to defensive retreats mirrors strategic situation
Ship availability reflects campaign momentum (access to advanced ships vs relegation to outdated models)
